result.markdown:
--- title: "Phase 9 — One- vs Two-Compartment Comparison" subtitle: "Structural model selection: is a peripheral compartment justified?" author: "Pramod BR" date: today format: html: theme: cosmo toc: true toc-depth: 3 code-fold: true number-sections: true fig-width: 8 fig-height: 6 execute: echo: true warning: false message: false --- ## Objectives The whole analysis assumed a **one-compartment** structure. Here we test that assumption directly by fitting a **two-compartment** model and asking whether the extra peripheral compartment is statistically justified. Demonstrating that a more complex model can be fitted *and correctly accepted or rejected* is core model-selection judgement. ## The two-compartment model A peripheral compartment lets drug distribute into tissues and return, producing a biphasic semilog decline. Two new parameters appear: **Q** (inter-compartmental clearance) and **V2** (peripheral volume); the central volume becomes **Vc**. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="false"} print(two_cmt_model) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` function () { ini({ tka <- log(1.5) tcl <- log(2.8) tvc <- log(32) tq <- log(2) tvp <- log(10) eta.ka ~ 0.3 eta.cl ~ 0.1 eta.vc ~ 0.1 add.sd <- 0.2 prop.sd <- 0.2 }) model({ ka <- exp(tka + eta.ka) cl <- exp(tcl + eta.cl) vc <- exp(tvc + eta.vc) q <- exp(tq) vp <- exp(tvp) d/dt(depot) <- -ka * depot d/dt(center) <- ka * depot - (cl/vc) * center - (q/vc) * center + (q/vp) * periph d/dt(periph) <- (q/vc) * center - (q/vp) * periph cp <- center/vc cp ~ add(add.sd) + prop(prop.sd) }) } ``` ::: ::: ## Fit and compare ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} dat <- build_nonmem_data() iiv_fit <- load_iiv_fit() two_fit <- fit_two_cmt_model(dat) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` calculating covariance matrix done ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} base_param_table(two_fit) |> kbl(caption = "Two-compartment model estimates", digits = 3) |> kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Two-compartment model estimates
Parameter Est. SE %RSE Back-transformed(95%CI) BSV(CV%) Shrink(SD)%
tka 0.259 0.214 82.9 1.3 (0.851, 1.97) 67.8 1.93%<
tcl 1.01 0.0785 7.76 2.75 (2.36, 3.21) 26.0 1.52%<
tvc 3.35 0.118 3.53 28.5 (22.6, 36) 14.8 15.6%<
tq 0.656 1.07 163 1.93 (0.237, 15.7)
tvp 1.3 0.809 62.1 3.68 (0.753, 18)
add.sd 0.278 0.278
prop.sd 0.131 0.131
````` ::: ::: The key diagnostic for *worth* is whether **Q** and **V2** are precisely estimated (reasonable %RSE) — if their relative standard errors are very large, the data cannot support a peripheral compartment. ## Model selection Adding two parameters is justified (p < 0.05) only if the OFV drops by more than **5.99** (χ², 2 df); AIC/BIC additionally penalise complexity. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} cmp <- compare_models( model_metrics(iiv_fit, "1-compartment (selected)"), model_metrics(two_fit, "2-compartment") ) cmp |> kbl(caption = "1- vs 2-compartment model comparison", digits = 2) |> kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
1- vs 2-compartment model comparison
Model OFV AIC BIC nPar dOFV dAIC
1-compartment (selected) 104.36 362.96 386.02 8 0.00 0.00
2-compartment 102.75 365.35 394.18 10 -1.61 2.39
````` ::: ::: Here ΔOFV = **-1.61** and ΔAIC = **2.39**. The two-compartment model is therefore **not supported**: the extra compartment does not improve the fit enough to justify two more parameters. ## Key takeaways - A full **two-compartment** oral model was fitted and compared head-to-head with the selected one-compartment model. - Decision by **ΔOFV / ΔAIC / BIC**: the two-compartment model is **rejected in favour of the simpler 1-compartment model**. - For theophylline, the one-compartment structure is well established, so rejecting the extra compartment is the expected and **parsimonious** result — confirming the structural choice made back in Phase 3. > Next (Phase 10): **bootstrap confidence intervals** on the final model to quantify > parameter uncertainty empirically. ```
